title: Therapeutic tnfr-1b targets and uses thereof
abstract: Disclosed are tumor necrosis factor receptor 1B (TNFR-1B) signaling targets and TNFR-1B mutants and their uses for treatment of diseases and disorders.
claims: Claims What is claimed is: 1. A mutant of tumor necrosis factor receptor IB (TNFR-IB) that modulates the binding of TNFR-IB to one or more of TNFa, ICOS-L and MadCAM-1, wherein the mutant is selected from the group consisting of mutants K42D, T48A, N171D, S79D, R113D, L114A, R119D, K120D, D58A, R19D, S59D, L64D, R77A, S107D, R119A, K120A, R129A, V138D, K140A, I156D, I168D, N171A and M174D. 2. The mutant of Claim 1, selected from the group consisting of mutants K42D, T48A and N171D that, compared to TNFR-IB, have reduced binding to TNFa, ICOS-L and MadC AM- 1. 3. The mutant of Claim 1, selected from the group consisting of mutants S79D, R113D, L114A, R119D and K120D that, compared to TNFR-IB, have reduce binding to ICOS-L and MadCAM-1, but not to TNFa. 4. The mutant of Claim 1, which is mutant D58A that, compared to TNFR-IB, has reduced binding to TNFa and ICOS-L, but not to MadCAM-1. 5. The mutant of Claim 1, selected from the group consisting of mutants R19D, S59D, L64D, R77A, S107D, R119A, K120A, R129A, V138D, K140A, I156D, I168D, N171A and M174D that, compared to TNFR-IB, predominately reduce binding to MadCAM-1. 6. A fusion protein comprising the mutant of Claim 1 and an immunoglobulin Fc sequence. 7. The fusion protein of Claim 6, wherein the immunoglobulin is an IgG. 8. The fusion protein of Claim 6, wherein the immunoglobulin is IgGl. 9. A pharmaceutical composition comprising the fusion protein of Claim 6 and a pharmaceutically acceptable carrier. 10. A method for treating a subject with a disease or disorder selected from the group consisting of rheumatoid arthritis, psoriatic arthritis, ankylosing spondylitis, plaque psoriasis, juvenile idiopathic arthritis, inflammation, autoimmune disease, immune disorder, Crohn's disease, ulcerative colitis and inflammatory bowel disease, the method comprising administering to the subject the fusion protein of Claim 6 in an amount effective to alleviate a sign or symptom of the disease or disorder. 11. A method for screening for a candidate compound for treating a disease or disorder selected from the group consisting of rheumatoid arthritis, psoriatic arthritis, ankylosing spondylitis, plaque psoriasis, juvenile idiopathic arthritis, inflammation, autoimmune disease, immune disorder, Crohn's disease, ulcerative colitis and inflammatory bowel disease, the method comprising testing the compound to determine if the compound modulates the interaction between one or more of TNFR-IB and ICOS-L, TNFR-IB and MadC AM- 1, TNFR-IB and ISLR2, and B7-1 and ISLR2, wherein a compound that is tested and determined to modulate the interaction between one or more of TNFR-IB and ICOS-L, TNFR-IB and MadCAM-1, TNFR-IB and ISLR2, and B7-1 and ISLR2 is a candidate compound for treating a disease or disorder selected from the group consisting of rheumatoid arthritis, psoriatic arthritis, ankylosing spondylitis, plaque psoriasis, juvenile idiopathic arthritis, inflammation, autoimmune disease, immune disorder, Crohn's disease, ulcerative colitis and inflammatory bowel disease. 12. The method of claim 11, wherein binding between TNFR-IB and ICOS-L, TNFR- IB and MadCAM-1, TNFR-IB and ISLR2, and/or B7-1 and ISLR2 is determined in the presence of the candidate compound and in the absence of the candidate compound, and wherein a change in the binding between TNFR-IB and ICOS-L, TNFR-IB and MadCAM- 1, TNFR-IB and ISLR2, and/or B7-1 and ISLR2 in the presence of the candidate compound indicates that the candidate compound modulates the interaction between TNFR-IB and ICOS-L, TNFR-IB and MadCAM-1, TNFR-IB and ISLR2, and/or B7-1 and ISLR2. 13. The method of Claim 11 or 12, wherein the compound is a non-naturally occurring small molecule of 2,000 daltons or less. 14. The method of Claim 11 or 12, wherein the compound is an antibody or an antibody fragment. 15. A method for screening for a candidate compound for treating a disease or disorder selected from the group consisting of rheumatoid arthritis, psoriatic arthritis, ankylosing spondylitis, plaque psoriasis, juvenile idiopathic arthritis, inflammation, autoimmune disease, immune disorder, Crohn's disease, ulcerative colitis and inflammatory bowel disease, the method comprising testing a modified TNFR-IB compound or mutant to determine if the modified TNFR-IB compound or mutant has modified binding affinity for one or more of ICOS-L, MadCAM-1 and ISLR2, wherein a modified TNFR-IB compound or mutant that is tested and determined to have modified binding affinity for one or more of ICOS-L, MadCAM-1 and ISLR2 is a candidate compound or mutant for treating a disease or disorder selected from the group consisting of rheumatoid arthritis, psoriatic arthritis, ankylosing spondylitis, plaque psoriasis, juvenile idiopathic arthritis, inflammation, autoimmune disease, immune disorder, Crohn's disease, ulcerative colitis and inflammatory bowel disease. 16. The method of Claim 15, wherein the modified TNFR-IB compound or mutant recognizes TNF-alpha, but not one or more of ICOS-L, MadCAM-1 and ISLR2. 17. The method of Claim 15, wherein the modified TNFR-IB compound or mutant recognizes ICOS-L, but not one or more of TNF-alpha, MadCAM-1 and ISLR2. 18. The method of Claim 15, wherein the modified TNFR-IB compound or mutant recognizes MadCAM-1, but not one or more of TNF-alpha, ICOS-L, and ISLR2. 19. The method of Claim 15, wherein the modified TNFR-IB compound or mutant recognizes ISLR2, but not one or more of TNF-alpha, ICOS-L, and MadCAM-1. 20. The method of Claim 15, wherein the modified TNFR-IB compound or mutant recognizes ICOS-L and MadCAM-1, but not TNF-alpha or ISLR2. 21. The method of any of Claims 1 1-15, wherein the compound or mutant recognizes only one ligand. 22. The method of any of Claims 11 -15, wherein the compound or mutant has enhanced affinities for all ligands compared to TNFR-IB. 23. The method of any of Claims 11 -15, wherein the compound or mutant has enhanced affinities for some ligands compared to TNFR-IB. 24. The method of any of Claims 11 -15, wherein the compound or mutant has reduced affinities for some ligands compared to TNFR-IB. 25. The method of any of Claims 11 -15, wherein the compound or mutant has reduced affinities for some ligands compared to TNFR-IB and enhanced affinities for some ligands compared to TNFR-IB.
